message:
fp-frame: extend DataFrame::align_on_index to handle duplicate labels

Use the duplicate-aware alignment path when aligning two DataFrames on
outer join and either side's index contains duplicate labels. Previously
DataFrame::align_on_index unconditionally called align(), which
produces a simple union and breaks the cartesian fan-out contract that
pandas uses when indexes repeat.

Behavior:
- If mode is AlignMode::Outer and either self.index.has_duplicates()
  or other.index.has_duplicates(), call align_union_duplicate_aware
  (the helper already used on the Series side and by DataFrame filter
  paths at lines 649/934/15585) to build the union_index,
  left_positions, and right_positions. These are assembled into an
  AlignmentPlan and run through the existing validate_alignment_plan
  gate so error reporting is identical to the regular path.
- All other cases (inner/left/right, or outer with unique indexes on
  both sides) continue to use the previous align() helper to keep the
  fast path untouched.

Test: dataframe_align_outer_duplicate_labels_cartesian
- df1 has column a=[10,20] at index [1,1]
- df2 has column b=[1,2]   at index [1,1]
- After align_on_index(Outer), both sides must have length 4 with index
  [1,1,1,1], left.a = [10,10,20,20] and right.b = [1,2,1,2]. That is
  the 2x2 cartesian expansion pandas produces for duplicate-label outer
  alignment.

The helper align_union_duplicate_aware is already defined at line 384
of the same file and is the canonical duplicate-aware union used by
the other DataFrame align paths in the crate, so this change just
wires DataFrame::align_on_index into that same path.
